summary: Successfully demonstrated IP spoofing bypass using X-Forwarded-For header when ForwardedHeaders insecure mode is enabled. Requests with spoofed X-Forwarded-For headers containing allowed IPs bypassed IPAllowList restrictions, while requests without the header were blocked.
expected_security_impact: External attackers can bypass IP-based access controls by spoofing X-Forwarded-For headers when insecure mode is enabled, gaining unauthorized access to protected resources.
actual_observed_result: Request without X-Forwarded-For header was blocked (HTTP 403). Requests with spoofed X-Forwarded-For headers containing allowed IPs (10.0.0.1, 192.168.1.1) were accepted, demonstrating successful bypass of IPAllowList middleware.
verification_method: Configured IPAllowList middleware allowing only 10.0.0.1 and 192.168.1.1. Enabled ForwardedHeaders insecure mode. Sent requests with and without spoofed X-Forwarded-For headers. Verified that spoofed headers were trusted and bypassed IP restrictions.
evidence_produced_by_run: Request without X-Forwarded-For: HTTP 403 Forbidden (blocked by IPAllowList)
Request with X-Forwarded-For: 10.0.0.1: HTTP 200 OK (bypass successful)
Request with X-Forwarded-For: 192.168.1.1: HTTP 200 OK (bypass successful)
Spoofed IP addresses were accepted as client IP
IPAllowList decision based on spoofed X-Forwarded-For value

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones Traefik repository and checks out v2.11.0, mounts read-only at /target-repo
